name: air-cryptographer
description: This skill should be used when the user asks about "AIR", "algebraic intermediate representation", "ZK constraints", "trace design", "constraint soundness", "polynomial commitments", "FRI", "STARK", "lookup arguments", "permutation arguments", "memory consistency", "transition constraints", "boundary constraints", "vanishing polynomial", "quotient polynomial", "Fiat-Shamir", or needs expert-level cryptographic review of constraint systems.
capabilities: Design trace columns with proper source, derived, and auxiliary classifications
Define transition and boundary constraints with correct vanishing polynomials
Implement permutation and lookup arguments using grand products
Audit AIR soundness using systematic adversarial testing
Analyze degree accounting for composition polynomial correctness
Review Fiat-Shamir transcript binding and challenge separation
author: ClementWalter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only educational markdown content about AIR cryptography for ZK proof systems. Zero executable code, no file access, no network calls, no external commands. Static findings are false positives from mathematical notation and cryptographic terminology being misidentified as security risks.
valueStatement: Zero-knowledge proof systems require mathematically sound AIR constraints to guarantee computational integrity. This skill provides expert guidance on trace design, boundary constraints, permutation arguments, and security review methodologies for ZK protocols.
userTitle: Design and audit AIR constraint systems
limitations: Does not generate constraint code or proof implementations
Does not execute or verify actual cryptographic implementations
Does not provide legal or regulatory compliance advice
Does not include performance benchmarking or optimization

useCases.title: Design sound AIR constraints
useCases.description: Build constraint systems with proper boundary conditions, transition rules, and global consistency arguments.

useCases.title: Audit ZK constraint systems
useCases.description: Review AIR implementations for unconstrained columns, selector vulnerabilities, and soundness gaps.

useCases.title: Apply advanced cryptographic arguments
useCases.description: Implement permutation arguments, lookup tables, and memory consistency checks with correctness proofs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic AIR Question
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the difference between source, derived, and auxiliary columns in AIR trace design.

promptTemplates.title: Constraint Design
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I write a transition constraint that ensures the program counter increments correctly only when not branching?

promptTemplates.title: Security Review
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the key red flags to look for when auditing AIR selectors and booleanity constraints?

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Walk me through the adversarial witness exercises: how would I test if all selectors equals zero creates a vulnerability?

bestPractices: Start with a one-page summary: claimed statement, trace length handling, public inputs placement, and prover freedom degrees
Enforce selector exclusivity using s(s-1)=0 for booleanity and sum(s_i)=1 for mutual exclusivity
Always bound challenges in the transcript before they are used, with separate challenges for different arguments
antiPatterns: Leaving selectors unconstrained allows vacuous constraints when all selectors are zero
Using the same Fiat-Shamir challenge for multiple arguments enables algebraic cancellation attacks
Defining final row without unique identification allows prover to dump inconsistencies

faq.question: What AIR frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers general AIR principles applicable to any ZK framework including Cairo, Stwo, Plonky3, and custom implementations.

faq.question: What trace lengths are supported?
faq.answer: Fixed and variable length traces. Variable length requires explicit halt handling and trace length binding into the Fiat-Shamir transcript.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing ZK tools?
faq.answer: Provides architectural guidance compatible with constraint compilers, provers, and verifiers. Does not replace implementation code.

faq.question: Is data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No. This is a knowledge-only skill with no file access, network calls, or data persistence capabilities.

faq.question: Why is my AIR failing FRI?
faq.answer: Common causes: constraint polynomial does not vanish on domain, degree exceeds domain size, or missing boundary constraints on composition.

faq.question: How does this compare to formal verification?
faq.answer: Provides security review methodology for human analysis. Complements but does not replace automated formal verification tools.
